channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mamelodi Sundowns advance to CAF Champions League semifinals despite red card
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns secured their place in the CAF Champions League semifinals after a tense second-leg encounter with Stade Malien in Bamako, Mali. Coming off a comfortable 3-0 home victory from the fi...

item.title: Mel Barrera-Montenegro’s Journey Highlights Resilience in ‘What Lies Beneath’
item.description: The latest ABS-CBN television drama series, “What Lies Beneath,” tells a compelling story of resilience and the enduring strength of women through the character of Mel Barrera-Montenegro. Played by Su...

item.title: US Counterterrorism Director Resigns Over Iran War, Citing Israeli Influence
item.description: In a significant development amid the ongoing US-Iran conflict, Joe Kent, director of the National Counterterrorism Center, resigned on March 18, 2026, becoming the first senior Trump administration o...

item.title: Kotak Mahindra Bank's Subsidiary Sells Nearly 31% Stake in Infina Finance for Rs 1,294 Crore
item.description: Kotak Mahindra Bank announced that its wholly-owned subsidiary, Kotak Mahindra Capital Company (KMCC), has entered into agreements to sell approximately 30.99% stake in Infina Finance Private Limited,...

item.title: Zanzibar Launches $300 Million Mangapwani Integrated Port to Tackle Port Congestion, Power and Fuel Storage Challenges
item.description: Zanzibar President Hussein Ali Mwinyi on Sunday, March 22, 2026, laid the foundation stone for the Mangapwani Integrated Port in North Unguja, a major $300 million (over Sh770 billion) project set to ...

item.title: Tinubu Vows to Tackle Insecurity as Key Priority
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has emphasized his administration's commitment to confronting the security challenges currently plaguing Nigeria. Speaking after hosting Vice President Kashim Shettima and ...

item.title: 67,800-Year-Old Hand Stencil in Sulawesi Cave Dated as World's Oldest Known Rock Art
item.description: Researchers from Griffith University, Indonesia's BRIN, and Southern Cross University have identified a hand stencil in Liang Metanduno cave on Muna Island, southeastern Sulawesi, Indonesia, as the ol...

item.title: Unexpected Kindness: A Birthday Fax Delivers Joy Across the World
item.description: This heartwarming story recounts a touching experience from the 1990s, when a woman travelling for work found herself feeling lonely on her birthday while staying in a Washington DC hotel. Her young d...

item.title: Benefits of Pretending Insanity in Kenyan Society
item.description: The article explores how some Kenyans gain rewards by acting mad, especially in a society that values cunning over honesty. It argues that pretending to be loco allows one to speak harsh truths withou...

item.title: Military experts question UK’s ability to defend against potential Iranian ballistic missile threats
item.description: Military experts have criticised the United Kingdom’s defences against ballistic missiles, claiming they are inadequate to protect the homeland from a potential attack by Iran. This assessment follows...

item.title: Mental health struggles among Generation X women in midlife
item.description: This article explores the growing mental health crisis among Generation X women, particularly those aged between 50 and 63. Despite being the first generation to benefit from free university education...

item.title: Forest secure crucial 3-0 victory over Spurs in relegation battle
item.description: Nottingham Forest claimed a vital 3-0 win against Tottenham Hotspur, moving out of the relegation zone in the Premier League. Despite Spurs dominating possession, they failed to make the most of their...

item.title: Blue Jays crush Rays 14-1 in spring training finale behind early power surge and strong pitching from Cease
item.description: In their final spring training matchup of 2026, the Toronto Blue Jays delivered a commanding 14-1 victory over the Tampa Bay Rays at TD Ballpark in Dunedin, Florida. The offensive explosion began imme...

item.title: PNP plans to raise women’s recruitment quota to strengthen leadership representation
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) is considering increasing the recruitment quota for women to 20 percent, reflecting the growing presence and influence of female officers in law enforcement. Accor...

item.title: Farmers mourn missed planting as subsidised fertiliser runs out in key counties
item.description: Wananchi wa kikulima kati ya mashirika makuu ya kilimo kama Trans Nzoia, Uasin Gishu na Elgeyo‑Marakwet wameanza kuhisi mafuta kwa sababu ya kushikwa kwa mbolea binafsi kwa ajili ya kusimamia msimu wa...

item.title: Iranian naval forces deploy asymmetric threats including submarines, speedboats and drones against US vessels in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The article details the naval threats posed by Iran to United States forces in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway for global oil and gas shipments that Iran has effectively closed. Iranian Revo...

item.title: Nigeria's Crude Oil Production Drops Amid Global Supply Disruptions
item.description: Nigeria's crude oil production has reached its lowest in 19 months, with an average of 1.483 million barrels daily in February 2026, including blended and unblended condensates. The latest production ...

item.title: Tanzania faces continued water shortage due to inefficiency, wastage, and infrastructure gaps
item.description: As Tanzania joins the global community to mark World Water Day 2026, experts have raised concern over the country’s ongoing water shortage despite progress made in expanding access. A 2024/25 report f...

item.title: How television technology is approaching the limits of human vision
item.description: Television displays have evolved dramatically from the bulky cathode ray tube (CRT) sets of the past to today’s ultra-thin and high-resolution screens. Early televisions used electron beams to light u...

item.title: Israeli ambassador declares Iran war will persist until regime collapse despite Trump’s signals of winding down
item.description: Tensions in the ongoing conflict between Israel, the United States, and Iran remain high, with Israel’s ambassador to Washington stating that military operations will continue until the Iranian regime...
